name: 36-1-demo-vs-production
prompt: Simple, cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: A split-screen scene. Left side shows a small stage with a laptop giving a perfect demo to a cheering audience of three people, confetti falling, everything working perfectly. Right side shows the same laptop now connected to hundreds of users, with error messages popping up like bubbles, gears grinding, and a stressed developer trying to hold everything together with tape and string. The style should be friendly and approachable, like a comic strip. No text or lettering in the image.

name: 36-2-role-spectrum
prompt: Simple, cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: A horizontal conveyor belt showing five friendly robot characters, each performing a different role. From left to right: a robot with a pencil drafting a document, a robot sorting items into labeled bins (classifier), a robot at a crossroads pointing directions (router), a robot with a magnifying glass examining books (researcher), and a robot with a checkmark shield inspecting output (verifier). Below the conveyor belt, a gradient bar goes from green (low autonomy, human nearby) to orange (high autonomy, human far away). No text or lettering in the image.

name: 37-1-observe-steer-loop
prompt: Simple, cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: Two contrasting development workflows. Top: a long waterfall with huge steps and a developer waiting months at each stage, looking bored with cobwebs forming. Bottom: a tight circular loop like a hamster wheel (but positive), where a happy developer rapidly cycles through describe, generate, observe, evaluate, steer steps, with evidence clipboards and test results flying around. A clock shows the top takes months while the bottom takes hours. No text or lettering in the image.

name: 37-3-docs-as-controls
prompt: Simple, cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: A developer sitting at a control panel shaped like an open book. The book's pages have become levers, dials, and switches. Each control is a different document type: a rulebook, a test checklist, a prompt template, a risk register, and a cost meter. Arrows flow from the controls to a friendly AI robot, showing how adjusting each document changes the robot's behavior. The robot's posture shifts based on which lever is pulled. No text or lettering in the image.

name: 37-5-three-pillars-bridge
prompt: Simple, cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: A three-legged bridge spanning from a rough workshop island (prototype) to a polished city island (production). Each bridge leg is labeled with an icon: a checkmark (functional readiness), a dashboard gauge (operational readiness), and a parachute (recovery readiness). One leg has cracks showing what happens when you skip it. A developer stands nervously in the middle, looking down at the gap. The style is humorous and slightly exaggerated. No text or lettering in the image.

name: 38-1-cost-engine
prompt: Simple, cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: A Rube Goldberg machine representing AI cost calculation. Four input funnels feed into the machine: a long scroll (system prompt tokens), a chatty parrot (verbose output), a stack of three models of different sizes with price tags (model tier selection), and a recycling symbol (caching discount). The machine processes these inputs and outputs a dollar bill, with two paths: one path produces a huge expensive bill, the other a tiny cheap one. A developer pulls a lever to switch between paths. No text or lettering in the image.

name: 38-3-cognitive-lockin
prompt: Simple, cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: Two growth curves shown as vines growing up a wall. The left vine (traditional vendor lock-in) grows slowly and steadily upward. The right vine (cognitive lock-in) starts thin but then explodes exponentially, wrapping around a developer's brain like a friendly but inescapable octopus. The developer looks surprised at how entangled they've become. A calendar on the wall shows time passing from 1 month to 6 months. At the bottom, a pair of scissors (switching cost) that could easily cut the left vine but is comically too small for the right one. No text or lettering in the image.

name: 37-4-trust-but-verify
prompt: Simple, cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: A human developer and an AI robot sitting at adjacent desks in a newsroom setting. The AI robot types frantically, producing stacks of code pages. The human developer acts as editor-in-chief, reviewing each page with a red pen and a magnifying glass, with a 'VERIFIED' stamp ready. Some pages get stamped and move to a 'ship' pile; others get a red X and go back to the robot. A trust meter between them shows a balanced, healthy middle position. The mood is collaborative, not adversarial. No text or lettering in the image.

name: 36-3-feasibility-funnel
prompt: Simple, cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: A large funnel at the top filled with colorful lightbulb ideas (dozens of them). The funnel has five filter layers, each acting as a sieve. At each layer, some lightbulbs pass through and others bounce off with red X marks. The filters get progressively finer. At the bottom, only two or three lightbulbs emerge into a green 'viable' zone, while a pile of rejected lightbulbs sits to the side looking slightly sad but not devastated. A product manager stands at the bottom catching the survivors. No text or lettering in the image.
